fields.bert_architecture_components: Multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder based on the original Vaswani et al. (2017) implementation (BERT states the implementation is almost identical to the original and refers readers to Vaswani et al. (2017) for an exhaustive background description of the model architecture, without listing individual sub-layers such as multi-head self-attention, position-wise feed-forward, sinusoidal position embeddings, residual connections, or layer norm explicitly in the cited passage).
fields.bert_transformer_role: Bidirectional Transformer (BERT = Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers; uses masked language model pre-training to fuse left and right context and pretrain a deep bidirectional Transformer).
fields.transformer_encoder_decoder_definition: Encoder stack: N = 6 identical layers, each with two sub-layers (multi-head self-attention and a positionwise fully connected feed-forward network), with a residual connection around each sub-layer followed by layer normalization. Decoder stack: N = 6 identical layers, each with the same two sub-layers as the encoder plus a third sub-layer that performs multi-head attention over the output of the encoder stack, and the self-attention sub-layer is modified (masked) to prevent positions from attending to subsequent positions, so predictions for position i can depend only on the known outputs at positions less than i.

markdown:
- **bert_transformer_role:** Bidirectional Transformer (BERT = Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers; uses masked language model pre-training to fuse left and right context and pretrain a deep bidirectional Transformer). [1][2]
- **bert_architecture_components:** Multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder based on the original Vaswani et al. (2017) implementation (BERT states the implementation is almost identical to the original and refers readers to Vaswani et al. (2017) for an exhaustive background description of the model architecture, without listing individual sub-layers such as multi-head self-attention, position-wise feed-forward, sinusoidal position embeddings, residual connections, or layer norm explicitly in the cited passage). [3]
- **transformer_encoder_decoder_definition:** Encoder stack: N = 6 identical layers, each with two sub-layers (multi-head self-attention and a positionwise fully connected feed-forward network), with a residual connection around each sub-layer followed by layer normalization. Decoder stack: N = 6 identical layers, each with the same two sub-layers as the encoder plus a third sub-layer that performs multi-head attention over the output of the encoder stack, and the self-attention sub-layer is modified (masked) to prevent positions from attending to subsequent positions, so predictions for position i can depend only on the known outputs at positions less than i. [4][5]

Sources
[1] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, Abstract, p. 1
[2] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 1 Introduction, p. 1
[3] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 3 BERT, p. 3
[4] Attention Is All You Need, 3.1 Encoder and Decoder Stacks, p. 3
[5] Attention Is All You Need, 3.1 Encoder and Decoder Stacks, p. 3
